business.headline: Sensex, Nifty open higher as IT stocks lead gains; investors await Q1 earningsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 184.38 points to 77,369.81, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 42.40 points to 24,120.90 as of 9:30 am.

EPFO credits FY26 interest to 34 crore accounts. Here's how to check your PF balanceEPFO has credited 8.25% FY26 interest to 34 crore member accounts by July 15. The early rollout, enabled by its centralised IT system, marks the fastest completion yet.

Diesel, jet fuel exports face higher tax as Brent crude stays above $85India has raised export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel after crude prices surged amid renewed US-Iran tensions. The move reflects supply concerns around key shipping routes and pressure from higher global oil prices.

Why Samsung and SK Hynix shares crashed up to 9% despite the AI boomSamsung Electronics and SK Hynix shares slumped as a global semiconductor sell-off spread from Wall Street to Asia. The fall reflected profit booking, valuation worries and fresh questions over the pace of AI spending despite firm chip demand.

China's economy slows to 4.3% in Q2 as domestic demand weakensOfficial data released by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) on Wednesday showed that the world's second-largest economy expanded by 4.3% between April and June, down from 5% in the first quarter.

Rs 62,500 crore push for mobile manufacturing: Will it make smartphones cheaper?The move is expected to help India strengthen its position as a global manufacturing hub and the scheme will run for five years, from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.

India-UK FTA goes live: Why UK High Commissioner calls it the 'new gold standard'UK High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron called the India-UK FTA the "new gold standard" for free trade agreements and said it should be viewed as the beginning of a deeper partnership rather than its final destination.

Sensex recovers but profit booking wipes out most gains amid oil concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex settled 130.49 points, or 0.17%, higher at 77,185.43 after climbing more than 500 points during the session. The NSE Nifty50 also erased a large part of its intraday gains to close 27.70 points, or 0.11%, higher at 24,078.50.

Centre approves Semicon 2.0 with Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlayThe Union Cabinet has approved Semicon 2.0 with a Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlay for chip design and manufacturing. The move seeks to expand India's role in the global semiconductor supply chain and reduce import dependence.

Files linked to Kudankulam N-power plant exposed in data breach: ReutersA ransomware group has published a large cache of stolen data on the dark web, claiming it includes blueprints of parts of India's largest nuclear power plant Kudankulam. The hackers said the data was obtained from Anil Ambani's Reliance Group, a contractor at the plant.

IBM lost over $100 billion in just 42 days. Should Indian IT investors worry?IBM's $100 billion market value erosion in 42 days has jolted technology stocks and raised questions for Indian IT. The sell-off reflects a wider shift towards AI infrastructure spending, though recent Indian IT earnings remain steadier.
Bihar to offer doorstep property registration for citizens aged 75 and aboveSenior citizens who wish to use the service must first book an appointment through Bihar's official e-registration portal.

A trade deal built for India's real economyFor most trade agreements, that gap between signature and implementation is a routine administrative window. For CETA, it has been the period in which the government has assembled the ground-level architecture that will determine whether the agreement delivers on its promise.

China's economy is slowing despite record exports. Here's what's going wrongChina's economy grew at its slowest pace in three years in the April-June quarter, even as exports surged. The gap underscores how a property slump, weak spending and job uncertainty are weighing on households.

Inside Fairfax's proposed $5.5 billion IDBI Bank acquisition and what's at stakeIf the proposed transaction goes through, it could be worth around $5.5 billion-$5.7 billion, making it one of the largest foreign investments ever in an Indian bank and one of the government's most significant privatisation deals in recent years.

Will your PF grow under India-UK social security pact? ExplainedWhile the India-UK trade pact has attracted attention for opening up markets and reducing tariffs, the social security agreement could have an equally meaningful impact for Indian professionals.

Top 10 changes as India-UK FTA kicks in: Cheaper cars, whisky, zero-duty exportsIndia's trade pact with the UK has come into effect, lowering duties and widening market access. The deal is expected to aid exporters, professionals and businesses while reshaping bilateral trade.

IBM's $70 billion wipeout: What triggered its worst stock crash in 58 years?IBM just suffered its worst stock crash in nearly six decades. What went wrong, and what does it reveal about the winners and losers of the AI race?
Dalal Street opens in green as banks lead gains, crude stays above $85The S&P BSE Sensex rose 530.01 points to 77,584.95, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 145.10 points to 24,197.15 as of 9:30 am.

Did AI help decide Meta's layoffs? Lawsuit makes a serious claimTwenty-six Meta employees have accused the company of using AI-assisted tools to score workers ahead of layoffs. The case could test how far employers can rely on such systems in workplace decisions.

entertainment.headline: The Odyssey craze: Pregnant fan puts hospital plans on hold to watch Nolan's epic Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has sparked extraordinary fan reactions, with one moviegoer revealing she delayed plans for a second child to attend its opening-day screening.

God or King? The fascinating divide between Jr NTR and Dhanush's Murugan films Jr NTR-Trivikram and Dhanush-Vetri Maaran have announced different Murugan films within days of each other. Their contrasting portrayals of Lord Murugan have sparked a wider debate on faith, history and Tamil identity.

Exclusive: Hannah Waddingham, Octavia Spencer on female friendships, action at 50 In a chat with India Today, actors Hannah Waddingham and Octavia Spencer spoke about leading an action series, honest female friendships and breaking age stereotypes in Hollywood. The two also shared why they hope Ride or Die opens more doors for women in the genre.

BTS turn fake scandal into viral campaign ahead of NORMAL music video release BTS launched a tabloid-style teaser campaign ahead of the NORMAL music video release. The stunt went viral as fans linked the ads to the group's wider ARIRANG rollout.

The Odyssey box office prediction: Rs 20 crore Day 1 and Nolan's new India chapter Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is tracking for a strong India opening, driven by brisk IMAX sales and heavy advance bookings. The response has reinforced India's growing weight for Hollywood and Nolan's rare draw as a director-led theatrical event.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 6: Ajay Devgn's film joins Rs 100 crore club in India Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 crossed the Rs 100-crore mark in India on its sixth day in theatres. The Ajay Devgn-starrer has now earned Rs 124.36 crore worldwide and continues its theatrical run.

The Odyssey explained: 3,000-year-old story behind Christopher Nolan's biggest film As Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey arrives in Indian cinemas, here's everything to know about Homer's 3,000-year-old epic, the legendary hero Odysseus and the timeless story that inspired the film.

Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana trailer gets U certificate; runtime out The trailer of Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana has received a U certificate ahead of its worldwide launch on July 24. The certification details reveal that two versions of the trailer have been approved.

Seen him go through heartbreak, cry: Aamir's friend amid third marriage backlash Aamir Khan's friend Amin Hajee defended the actor amid backlash over his third marriage, saying he has witnessed his heartbreak and tears. He also praised Aamir for staying honest in his relationships and treating marriage with dignity.

I Play Rocky trailer: Anthony Ippolito turns Sylvester Stallone in underdog story Actor Anthony Ippolito has portrayed actor Sylvester Stallone in director Peter Farrelly's biographical drama I Play Rocky. The film revisits Stallone's fight to play Rocky Balboa and the making of the 1976 hit.

Never come for me: Mouni Roy's fiery reaction to paps filming her inside car. Video Actor Mouni Roy objected to paparazzi filming her after she got into her car in Mumbai. She later said in a deleted social media post that she had not called them and did not want to be photographed.

The Batman 2: Robert Pattinson's first look revealed with new release date Actor Robert Pattinson's first look in The Batman 2 has been revealed with the actor in his batsuit. Director Matt Reeves also used the announcement to share the film's revised release date.

Actor Shehnaaz Gill cheers for 'good friend' Raghav Juyal, shuts down dating rumours Actor Shehnaaz Gill declined to address rumours linking her with Raghav Juyal and instead promoted his upcoming Bhai Tera Star Hai. The speculations about their relationship started

Anurag Kashyap stands by Sonam Wangchuk, calls the government's silence deafening Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has backed Sonam Wangchuk as the activist continues his hunger strike for 18th day. His post sharpened focus on the government's silence as concerns grow over Wangchuk's health.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026 Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reimagines Odysseus' journey home as a vast modern epic. The film pairs mythic scale with the emotional toll of war, family separation and survival.

Actor Sivakarthikeyan meets CM Vijay ahead of Jana Nayagan release Actor Sivakarthikeyan met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and actor Vijay ahead of Jana Nayagan's release and shared photographs from their meeting. In his note, he praised Vijay's journey and described their relationship as a continuing bond of brotherhood.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan overseas bookings open; Canada prioritises VIP members Advance ticket sales for Vijay's final film Jana Nayagan has started in several overseas markets, with Canada adopting a phased booking system that gives early access to VIP members.

Haiwaan: Akshay Kumar, Saif Ali Khan unleash vengeance in gritty first look The first look of Haiwaan hints at a sharp face-off between actors Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan. The film also marks director Priyadarshan's official remake of his 2016 Malayalam film, Oppam.

No green screens, real ships, 91 days: How Nolan spent $250 million on The Odyssey The documentary The Odyssey: The Making of an Epic tracks Christopher Nolan's $250 million production across five countries without relying on green screens. It highlights a practical filmmaking approach shaped by real locations, physical training and historically rooted sound design.

general news.headline: I'm not in good shape: Sonam Wangchuk enters day 19 of indefinite hunger strikeSonam Wangchuk, who entered the 19th day of his indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar on Thursday, said he was "not in good shape but not so bad either" as doctors kept him under round-the-clock medical supervision after his weight dropped to 57.15 kg as of July 15.

Messi and Argentina punish timid England to storm into another World Cup finalEngland looked destined for the final after Anthony Gordon's opener, but Argentina once again found a way. Enzo Fernandez's stunning equaliser and Lautaro Martinez's late winner, inspired by Lionel Messi, sealed a dramatic comeback and sent the defending champions into back-to-back FIFA World Cup finals.

India has ended up with 700 crore litres of extra ethanol capacity. What now?India raced to build ethanol production capacity, and achieved an E20 mandate five years ahead of schedule. Now, the big question is whether India has built more production capacity than its current demand. What happens to the notional surplus capacity of approximately 700 crore litres?

Tuchel's decisions cost us: England legends slam head coach for semi-final blunderFIFA World Cup 2026: Wayne Rooney led the criticism of Thomas Tuchel after England's 2-1 World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, saying the manager's tactical decisions "cost us" the match. Shearer, Neville, Richards and Owen also questioned England's passive approach.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reimagines Odysseus' journey home as a vast modern epic. The film pairs mythic scale with the emotional toll of war, family separation and survival.

India's first consumer court order on E20 goes in Chhattisgarh vehicle owner's favourThe Raipur consumer commission ruled for a vehicle owner who blamed E20 petrol for recurring engine trouble. The order highlights fuel choice constraints and fixes liability for repair costs and compensation.

OnePlus to shut shop in US, Europe and India soon, report saysOnePlus may soon be winding down its operations in the US, Europe, and India soon. As per a new report, the company may announce plans to shut shop in select markets as early as this week.

Doctor murdered, 8-year-old son stabbed. Cops find wife scrolling phone by bodiesDr Kiran Honannavar was allegedly killed by his doctor wife inside their apartment in Karnataka, while their eight-year-old son was also allegedly stabbed. A video from the scene showed Dr Honannavar's wife lying down and scrolling through her phone while the bodies of her husband and son lay in pools of blood.

Is Messi really 39? Ageless, tireless and still football's greatest conductorAt 39, Lionel Messi no longer overwhelms football with pace. He now bends matches to his rhythm, turning intelligence into Argentina's greatest weapon. England discovered it the hard way. Spain are next to try and stop football's ultimate orchestrator.

I spent a night as a cockroach at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest. This is what I sawI stayed overnight at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar without revealing my identity. I spoke to the participants, ate the food being served and overheard the conversations around fasting activist Sonam Wangchuk. I also noticed the unease over absent CJP leaders.

Rs 5 lakh bribe to paper-setter, 111 questions matched: CBI in NEET leak caseThe CBI told a special court that a Latur coaching centre owner allegedly paid Rs 5 lakh for leaked NEET chemistry questions. The claim strengthens the prosecution's case as the agency opposes bail and cites matched handwritten notes.

'Are you Muslim?' Indian man stabbed 15 times over religion at US mallAn Indian Muslim man was critically injured in a suspected hate-motivated stabbing inside a Utah mall, prompting the Indian Consulate in San Francisco to extend consular assistance to his family.

When Dharmendra Pradhan met Sonam WangchukWhen Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Ladakh-based activist and educationist Sonam Wangchuk held a meeting in 2023 over the National Education Policy, it reflected warmth and optimism. Three years later, Wangchuk is on an indefinite hunger strike, demanding Pradhan's resignation.

Argentina celebrate England win with Falklands banner. Is it breach of FIFA rules?FIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's post-match celebrations after beating England in the World Cup semi-final took a political turn as players displayed a "Las Malvinas Son Argentinas" banner. The incident could draw FIFA scrutiny over rules banning political messages inside stadiums.

Argentina didn't give people a chance to talk crap: Lionel Messi after England winFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi said Argentina denied critics the chance to "talk crap" after their 2-1 comeback win over England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final. The captain also said the reigning champions always believed they were the better football team.

Sonam Wangchuk in critical condition, Centre responsible if anything happens: TMCTMC urged Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan to break his silence and open talks with Sonam Wangchuk, warning the activist's health had turned critical after 18 days on hunger strike.

Video: Society row turns ugly as former office-bearers fight inside Ghaziabad liftTwo former AOA office-bearers were seen fighting inside a lift at Ghaziabad's GS-7 society. The viral CCTV clip has triggered a police inquiry and concern among residents.

Kidney transplant or euthanasia: 5 new mothers in Rajasthan refuse dialysisThe women allege their kidneys were damaged due to medical negligence and suspected spurious medicines during delivery, a charge the hospital denies. Hospital authorities say the women are stable, are receiving free treatment, and that it is too early under medical protocol to decide whether kidney transplants will be required.

POK on edge as showdown looms: 12 killed, 4,000 Rangers deployed, media blacked outCities and towns in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (POK) have been sealed, and an undeclared media blackout has been imposed on Rawalakot ahead of the march to Muzaffarabad on Wednesday. At least 12 people were killed in Tuesday's clashes as the Pakistani establishment stepped up its crackdown.

Monsoon warming up in Bay of Bengal after power nap. When will it rain?Something brewing in the Bay of Bengal is expected to revive the monsoon in coming days, bringing widespread rain to several states. Read when rain will arrive and where.

The Odyssey explained: 3,000-year-old story behind Christopher Nolan's biggest filmAs Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey arrives in Indian cinemas, here's everything to know about Homer's 3,000-year-old epic, the legendary hero Odysseus and the timeless story that inspired the film.

UP man crosses Pak border without visa for Facebook lover, lands in jailAn Aligarh man who entered Pakistan without a visa to meet a woman he knew on Facebook remains lodged in Lahore's Kot Lakhpat Jail. His family says he has completed his sentence and is still awaiting repatriation.

Dutch woman thought Mumbai was her dream city, until Bengaluru changed her mindA Dutch woman shared why moving to Mumbai from Bengaluru no longer matched the life she needed after becoming a mother. Her video started a conversation on how cities can feel different as priorities shift with life stages.

Russia seeks more petrol from India as Ukrainian strikes cripple oil refineriesAmong the Russian companies that have reportedly reached out are Rosneft, Gazprom Neft and Lukoil. The sources told Reuters that discussions have taken place with both private and government-owned refiners in India.

sports.headline: Lionel Messi's reaction to Jude Bellingham provocation is unmissable: Watch video Jude Bellingham received a chilling reminder from Argentina fans not to mess with Lionel Messi, as the England midfielder's spat with the football legend went viral during the semi-final of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Hope I'm never that stupid: Romero slams Gary Neville after Argentina's win FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristian Romero hit back at Gary Neville after Argentina's 2-1 World Cup semi-final win over England, saying he hopes he is never "that stupid" after the former England defender criticised Argentina's centre-back pairing before the match.

No doubt Messi is the greatest: Lionel Scaloni believes GOAT debate is now over Lionel Scaloni declared Lionel Messi the greatest footballer in history after Argentina's 2-1 semi-final comeback over England. Messi's two assists sent the holders into another World Cup final and to the edge of history.

Lionel Messi dedicates England win as a special gift to the late Diego Maradona Lionel Messi dedicated Argentina's dramatic World Cup semi-final win over England to Diego Maradona, saying the late legend would be "enjoying it from up above". The captain called the comeback victory "a gift" for Argentina's 1986 World Cup hero.

Argentina didn't give people a chance to talk crap: Lionel Messi after England win FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi said Argentina denied critics the chance to "talk crap" after their 2-1 comeback win over England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final. The captain also said the reigning champions always believed they were the better football team.

Is Messi really 39? Ageless, tireless and still football's greatest conductor At 39, Lionel Messi no longer overwhelms football with pace. He now bends matches to his rhythm, turning intelligence into Argentina's greatest weapon. England discovered it the hard way. Spain are next to try and stop football's ultimate orchestrator.

Tuchel's decisions cost us: England legends slam head coach for semi-final blunder FIFA World Cup 2026: Wayne Rooney led the criticism of Thomas Tuchel after England's 2-1 World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, saying the manager's tactical decisions "cost us" the match. Shearer, Neville, Richards and Owen also questioned England's passive approach.

Argentina celebrate England win with Falklands banner. Is it breach of FIFA rules? FIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's post-match celebrations after beating England in the World Cup semi-final took a political turn as players displayed a "Las Malvinas Son Argentinas" banner. The incident could draw FIFA scrutiny over rules banning political messages inside stadiums.

Thomas Tuchel has no regrets despite England's World Cup heartbreak vs Argentina Thomas Tuchel defended his tactical decisions after England's heartbreaking World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, insisting he had "no regrets" despite late criticism over his substitutions as the Three Lions surrendered a one-goal lead to miss out on a place in the final.

Messi and Argentina punish timid England to storm into another World Cup final England looked destined for the final after Anthony Gordon's opener, but Argentina once again found a way. Enzo Fernandez's stunning equaliser and Lautaro Martinez's late winner, inspired by Lionel Messi, sealed a dramatic comeback and sent the defending champions into back-to-back FIFA World Cup finals.

ENG 1-2 ARG Highlights: Argentina make late comeback to reach the final England (ENG) vs Argentina (ARG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final: (ENG 1-2 ARG) England came within touching distance of the FIFA World Cup final before Argentina produced another trademark comeback. Fernandez's equaliser and Lautaro Martinez's late winner, both inspired by Lionel Messi's brilliance, sent the defending champions into a mouthwatering final against Spain while leaving England heartbroken.

Ben Stokes returns to Durham with 'new lease of life' after England retirementBen Stokes returns to Durham for the first time since retiring from international cricket, beginning a new chapter far from England's spotlight. The One-Day Cup opener offers the all-rounder a chance to rediscover the joy of playing where it all began.

Axar Patel makes big jump in ICC ODI rankings after Birmingham heroicsAxar Patel has climbed three places to ninth in the ICC ODI all-rounders' rankings after his match-winning display of 4/62 and an unbeaten 57 in India's six-wicket victory over England in Birmingham.

BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia to head ICC's Governance Review CommitteeBCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has been appointed head of the ICC's newly formed Governance Review Committee and has also been included in the Franchise Leagues Committee led by former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal.

Carlos Alcaraz completely healed from injury, US Open return hopes brightenCarlos Alcaraz has made a full recovery from the wrist injury that sidelined him for nearly four months, with reports claiming that his medical team is satisfied with his progress ahead of a possible US Open return.

IPL-style eliminator, Super 7: ICC announces major changes to ODI, T20 World CupsThe ICC's major tournament revamp introduces a unified Super 7 round-robin stage for the men's ODI World Cup and brings high-stakes, IPL-style crossover eliminators to an expanded Super 10 phase in the T20 World Cup. We have tried to explain the new formats. Read on.

How to watch England vs Argentina World Cup semis: Date, time, streaming detailsEngland and Argentina will renew one of football's fiercest rivalries in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final in Atlanta, with the winners advancing to face Spain in Sunday's final.

Rohit Sharma gets coach Gautam Gambhir's backing amid ODI World Cup speculationIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir has backed Rohit Sharma to continue playing his natural game, with the team management keen to free the veteran opener from any pressure over his future.

I am ready: Smriti Mandhana prepared to replace Harmanpreet as India captainSmriti Mandhana says she is ready to captain India in Harmanpreet Kaur's absence, with the vice-captain boasting an impressive record of 15 wins in 22 matches across T20Is and ODIs.

India falters in Tokyo: Lakshya and Ayush knocked out in first roundLakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty joined India's early exits at the Japan Open after first-round defeats on Wednesday. PV Sindhu is the lone Indian shuttler to reach the second round of singles in Tokyo.

Selfless template gone, India back to old-fashioned grind ahead of 2027 World CupIndia's commanding win over England in the first ODI was more than just a morale-boosting result after the T20I debacle. It also offered the clearest indication yet that Shubman Gill's India are embracing a more patient, calculated ODI template with the 2027 World Cup in South Africa firmly in mind.

Freeze Kane: Argentina fans pin hopes on bizarre superstition before England clashArgentina fans are sticking to their cherished cabalas ahead of the World Cup semi-final against England, believing rituals such as freezing Harry Kane's name and repeating matchday routines can help the defending champions reach another final.

Teenage swagger: At 19, Lamine Yamal walks the talk, escapes the trolls in styleLamine Yamal's pre-match swagger was vindicated as Spain beat France 2-0 in the World Cup semi-final. His sharp display and Spain's control left the pre-tournament favourites chasing shadows.

IND vs ENG: Sundar credits coach Gambhir for unlocking his batting potentialWashington Sundar credited India head coach Gautam Gambhir and Gujarat Titans coach Ashish Nehra for helping him evolve as a batter after his unbeaten half-century guided India to a six-wicket victory over England in the first ODI at Edgbaston on Tuesday.

Atlanta Police tighten security for England vs Argentina World Cup semi-finalThe Atlanta Police Department (APD), in a statement on Tuesday, said it had enhanced security measures for Wednesday's FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final between England and Argentina at Atlanta Stadium, deploying additional personnel and resources to ensure the match passes off safely.

Stop Messi at all costs? England coach Tuchel considers masterplan for semi-finalEngland coach Thomas Tuchel said he has considered using an old-school man-marking plan to contain Lionel Messi when his side face Argentina in Wednesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final.

FIFA chief Infantino under IOC scrutiny over Balogun World Cup ban reversalThe International Olympic Committee (IOC) has received a complaint seeking an investigation into FIFA president Gianni Infantino's alleged role in reversing Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension. It also accuses Infantino of repeatedly breaching political neutrality rules through his support for US President Donald Trump.

Argentina vs England: Coach Scaloni outlines formula to reach World Cup finalArgentina coach Lionel Scaloni has urged his players to combine their trademark grit with their attacking quality when they take on England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final on Wednesday, insisting that determination alone will not be enough to secure a place in the final.

England vs Argentina semifinal guide: Messi steps into football's fiercest rivalryEngland vs Argentina semi-final: England and Argentina do not meet as ordinary opponents. Rooted in Maradona's infamous 1986 quarter-final, this rivalry carries weight few international fixtures can match. Now Messi faces the one team that has always eluded him with a spot in the final against Spain up for grabs.

Lionel Messi's perfect-record referee to officiate Argentina vs England semi-finalFIFA has appointed Ismail Elfath to officiate Argentina's World Cup semi-final against England in Atlanta. Messi has been unbeaten in all five matches involving the Moroccan-American referee.

How Pedri and Lamine Yamal manifested Spain's World Cup final into existencePedri and Lamine Yamal's old Instagram post has resurfaced after Spain reached the World Cup final on 19 July. What once seemed cheeky now reflects the belief driving Spain's young side into a record-chasing title match.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
